sections.content:
Integers

Integers are whole numbers — positive, negative, or zero: …−3, −2, −1, 0, 1, 2, 3… When adding and subtracting integers, think about a number line. Moving right means adding, moving left means subtracting.

Rules for signs:
• (+) + (+) = positive → 4 + 3 = 7
• (−) + (−) = negative → −4 + (−3) = −7
• Adding a negative is the same as subtracting: 5 + (−3) = 5 − 3 = 2

For multiplication and division:
• Same signs → positive result: (−3) × (−4) = 12
• Different signs → negative result: (−3) × 4 = −12
🔑 Tip: Two negatives multiplied together always make a positive. Remember: minus times minus equals plus.

Fractions

A fraction represents part of a whole. The numerator (top) tells how many parts you have; the denominator (bottom) tells how many equal parts the whole is divided into.

Adding/Subtracting fractions: You must have a common denominator first.
Example: ½ + ¼ → convert ½ = 2/4, then 2/4 + 1/4 = 3/4.

Multiplying fractions: Multiply numerators together and denominators together.
Example: ⅔ × ¾ = (2×3)/(3×4) = 6/12 = ½

Dividing fractions: Flip the second fraction and multiply (Keep, Change, Flip).
Example: ¾ ÷ ½ = ¾ × 2/1 = 6/4 = 1½

Mixed Numbers and Improper Fractions

A mixed number has a whole part and a fraction part: 2¾. An improper fraction has a numerator larger than the denominator: 11/4. To convert 2¾ → improper: (2×4)+3 = 11, so 11/4. To convert 11/4 → mixed: 11÷4 = 2 remainder 3, so 2¾.

questions.question: Calculate: −6 + (−4)
questions.explanation: Adding two negatives gives a bigger negative: −6 + (−4) = −10.

questions.question: Calculate: ⅔ + ¼
questions.explanation: Common denominator of 3 and 4 is 12. ⅔ = 8/12 and ¼ = 3/12. So 8/12 + 3/12 = 11/12.

questions.question: Convert 3⅖ to an improper fraction
questions.explanation: Multiply the whole number by the denominator and add the numerator: (3×5)+2 = 17. So 3⅖ = 17/5.

questions.question: Calculate: ¾ ÷ ½
questions.explanation: Keep, Change, Flip: ¾ ÷ ½ = ¾ × 2/1 = 6/4 = 1½.
